prompt:
Ops page from the turnpike back office. The month-end statement batch renders one statement file per plaza feed, and the render step has an internal budget of 2 seconds per feed — that used to be laughably generous. This month the TP-Pass program crossed 180k crossings on the main feed and the render step alone is now taking north of 20 seconds; the whole batch window slipped and finance noticed. Small feeds still render instantly, which is why nobody caught it in staging.

The renderer is StatementReport.generate(List<Crossing>) in this directory. Its OUTPUT IS CONTRACT: the archive stores a checksum of every statement file ever rendered, and re-renders must be byte-identical — same feed in, same bytes out. Statement layout: one block per account with accounts in sorted order, each crossing on its own line in feed order within the account's block, a SUBTOTAL line per account with trip count, and a grand TOTAL line. Money renders as $D.CC. All of that stays exactly as it is.

Make generate() fit the month-end feed inside the budget without changing a single output byte. TestMain.java in this directory is the acceptance contract: the layout tests pin the bytes (including a checksummed 500-crossing feed), and the budget test builds the 180k-crossing month-end feed up front, times ONLY the generate() call via System.nanoTime, and pins the report's length and SHA-256 against the archive. Don't touch TestMain.java, and don't change the Crossing record. Done = `java TestMain.java` prints all PASS (including the budget test) and exits 0.
